negative_cases.why_wrong: Idt Voltage Integrator Source: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Accumulate the time integral of V(in) into the output response.

Idt Voltage Integrator Source: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use idt() to form a voltage-domain integrator source fail.
Idt Voltage Integrator Source: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Idt Voltage Integrator Source: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Accumulate the time integral of V(in) into the output response fails.

Idt Voltage Integrator Source: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use idt() to form a voltage-domain integrator source fail.
